name: Leaning Tower of Pisa
overview: The Leaning Tower of Pisa is the freestanding bell tower of the cathedral in Pisa, Italy. Famous worldwide for its unintended tilt, it's one of the most recognizable architectural structures in the world.
history: Construction began in 1173 and took place over three stages, completing in 1372. The tower began leaning during construction due to soft ground on one side. Various efforts to correct the tilt have been made throughout history.
highlights: Iconic lean of 3.97 degrees from vertical
294 steps spiral staircase to the top
Beautiful Romanesque architecture with blind arcades
Part of the stunning Piazza dei Miracoli (Square of Miracles)
tips: Book climbing tickets in advance - limited to 30 people every 30 minutes. Wear comfortable shoes for the spiral stairs. Don't forget the classic 'pushing the tower' photo opportunity!
